commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow automation repair can update the config baseline; after that, the remaining blocker is maintainer product/security sign-off.; Needs attention: No dependency or supply-chain change is apparent, but the opt-in feature creates a transcript-disclosure boundary that maintainers should explicitly accept.; Review finding: [P2] Regenerate the config docs baseline for sessionTitle — src/config/zod-schema.ts:7... (sha=bfab6d3bd5bf9e730b121254344e399af7b43b01)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds opt-in AI-generated session titles, persists `autoTitle`, projects `derivedTitle` into session listings and Control UI display, and extends internal runtime auth selector handling for title-generation model calls.

PR surface: Source +345, Tests +310. Total +655 across 23 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; current main source shows heuristic title derivation, and the PR body supplies after-change browser/gateway proof for the requested feature.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automation repair can update the config baseline; after that, the remaining blocker is maintainer product/security sign-off.

target.title: fix(sessions): auto-generate session titles via AI summarization
target.body:
## Summary

- Caps transcript excerpts before background session-title model calls: at most 1,000 chars per user message and 4,000 chars total are sent to the title model.
- Keeps plugin runtime `modelAuth.getRuntimeAuthForModel` from accepting or forwarding plugin-supplied credential selectors (`agentDir`, `profileId`, `preferredProfile`, `lockedProfile`, or `store`).
- Preserves the existing root fixes for complete JSONL transcript scanning, canonical session model routing, and skipping already titled sessions before any transcript read/model call.
- Adds focused regression coverage for the new title input cap and plugin auth wrapper selector stripping.

Fixes #77165

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Session title generation must still trigger when the first user JSONL record is very long and later user turns satisfy `sessionTitle.turnsBeforeTitle`, but the background title model must receive only a bounded transcript excerpt. Plugin runtime auth wrappers must not let plugin callers steer model credential selection through internal selector fields.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Users still get useful generated titles for long-prompt sessions, while opt-in title generation has a smaller transcript disclosure surface and plugins cannot choose private credential profiles through the runtime facade.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node v22.22.0, current PR worktree. The browser proof used the real gateway-served Control UI on loopback port 19034 with an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`. The successful model endpoint was a local OpenAI-compatible mock provider (`mock/mock-title`) because the available external ZTE provider returned 401 and the DeepSeek provider returned 402 insufficient balance in this environment.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
TASK_WORKTREE=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87643 \
EVIDENCE_DIR=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-87643-evidence \
bash /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-87643-evidence/start-round4-ui-mock-gateway.sh

TASK_WORKTREE=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87643 \
EVIDENCE_DIR=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-87643-evidence \
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-dev-pr87643-ui-mock/.openclaw-dev \
OPENCLAW_UI_URL=http://127.0.0.1:19034/ \
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=proof-token-87643-ui-mock \
OPENCLAW_PROOF_PROVIDER_NOTE="local OpenAI-compatible mock provider (mock/mock-title) used because external ZTE returned 401 and DeepSeek returned 402 in this environment" \
node /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-87643-evidence/run-round4-ui-proof.mjs

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/plugins/runtime/index.test.ts \
  src/plugins/runtime/runtime-model-auth.runtime.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/session-title-generator.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/conversation-label-generator.test.ts

pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm deadcode:ci
pnpm check:test-types
pnpm lint --threads=8
git diff --check
```
- **Evidence after fix:**
```text
round4-ui-proof.md (generated 2026-06-03T04:17:26.968Z):
- Control UI page opened: http://127.0.0.1:19034/
- sessionTitle config after patch: {"enabled":true,"turnsBeforeTitle":3,"maxChars":50}
- First user message chars: 11297
- Chat turns completed: 1:ok, 2:ok, 3:ok
- Durable autoTitle: Nebula Banana Notebook
- sessions.list derivedTitle: Nebula Banana Notebook

round5-focused-tests.log:
- Test Files  2 passed (2) / Tests  11 passed (11) for auto-reply shard
- Test Files  2 passed (2) / Tests  26 passed (26) for plugins shard
- passed 2 Vitest shards in 51.02s

round5-check-test-types.log:
- pnpm tsgo:test completed successfully

round5-lint.log:
- oxlint:core finished
- oxlint:extensions finished
- oxlint:scripts finished

round5-diff-check.log:
- passed with no output

round6-verification.md / round6-deadcode-ci.log:
- Synced to current origin/main after `check-main-drift` required `sync-main`.
- The previous remote `check-dependencies` failure was from Knip unused-file findings for pending agent SQLite scaffold files that were not in this PR diff.
- Current main contains `58160094e82e52a0d1f0ff9036e7806ac2ae02a4` (`fix: allowlist pending agent sqlite scaffold (#89705)`), which allowlists those exact files.
- After sync-main, `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` and `pnpm deadcode:ci` completed successfully.

round6-focused-tests.log / round6-check-test-types.log / round6-lint.log / round6-diff-check.log:
- 4 focused Vitest files / 37 tests passed.
- `pnpm check:test-types` completed successfully.
- `pnpm lint --threads=8` completed successfully.
- `git diff --check` passed with no output.
```
- **Observed result after fix:** A real browser opened the Control UI, connected through the page's `GatewayBrowserClient`, confirmed `sessionTitle.enabled`, created a session, sent one 11,297-character first user message plus two later turns, waited for all three agent runs, confirmed each assistant reply marker in `chat.history`, observed durable `autoTitle: Nebula Banana Notebook`, fetched `sessions.list` with `derivedTitle: Nebula Banana Notebook`, and captured a screenshot showing `Nebula Banana Notebook` in the Control UI recent session list. Focused unit tests prove the title model input is capped, long-first-record counting still reaches the title threshold, runtime session model routing still works, manual/subject-titled sessions do not disclose transcript excerpts, and plugin runtime auth wrappers strip credential selectors.
- **What was not tested:** The successful browser proof used a local OpenAI-compatible mock provider rather than a paid external provider because available ZTE and DeepSeek credentials failed with auth/billing errors. No live Mantis/Desktop proof was captured. The mock proof still exercises the real gateway, Control UI, WebSocket RPC client, chat send/wait/history path, title generator, durable session store, and `sessions.list` display path.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** This author update addresses the outstanding waiting-on-author work; the label itself is controlled by maintainers/bots.
- **RF-002 / RF-006:** `session-title-generator.ts` now builds a bounded title-model excerpt before calling `generateConversationLabel`: max 1,000 chars per user message and max 4,000 chars total. `session-title-generator.test.ts` now proves both long-first-record threshold counting and the total input cap.
- **RF-003 / RF-004:** Broader sign-off remains a maintainer decision. This update keeps `sessionTitle.enabled` explicit opt-in, narrows the transcript excerpt sent to the title model, and documents the selected cap and remaining external-provider/Mantis proof gap.
- **RF-005:** `createRuntimeModelAuth()` no longer forwards plugin-supplied `agentDir`, `profileId`, `preferredProfile`, or `lockedProfile` into `getRuntimeAuthForModel`; the public plugin runtime type now exposes only `model`, optional `cfg`, and `workspaceDir` for that facade. `index.test.ts` proves `agentDir`, `profileId`, `preferredProfile`, `lockedProfile`, and `store` are stripped.
- **RF-007:** Ran the requested focused Vitest command covering `src/plugins/runtime/index.test.ts`, `src/plugins/runtime/runtime-model-auth.runtime.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/session-title-generator.test.ts`, and `src/auto-reply/reply/conversation-label-generator.test.ts`; 4 files / 37 tests passed.
- **RF-008:** `git diff --check` passed with no output.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: compatibility`, `merge-risk: auth-provider`, `merge-risk: security-boundary`, plus session-state and message-delivery surfaces in the diff.
- **Risk explanation:** The broader PR adds a default-off `sessionTitle` config surface, durable `autoTitle` metadata, and background title model calls. This update narrows the background title input, uses the session's canonical selected model, fixes the long-record scanner, and prevents plugin runtime callers from steering credential selectors through the model auth facade.
- **Why acceptable:** Generation still requires `sessionTitle.enabled: true`; manual `displayName`, channel `subject`, and existing `autoTitle` prevent any title-model transcript read; title input is capped before the model call; the scanner uses complete transcript lines; and real Control UI proof confirms the gateway/session-list display path. Maintainers still own final sign-off on the broader config/privacy/auth-provider boundaries.

## Maintainer-ready confidence

- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for this contributor update: the current author-side code blockers are fixed, real Control UI proof is refreshed, the prior remote `check-dependencies` failure was attributed to a mainline unused-file allowlist gap and passes after `sync-main`, and focused tests, test typecheck, lint, and diff whitespace checks pass. The broader merge decision still needs maintainer sign-off for the existing config/privacy/auth-provider risk labels.
- **Patch quality notes:** The latest production changes are narrow: cap the existing title-model excerpt, remove plugin-facing credential selector forwarding, use the existing canonical session model resolver, return before transcript reads for already titled sessions, and keep complete-line transcript scanning for long JSONL records. The existing broad catches/default returns are intentional non-blocking behavior for a background title feature: failed title generation must not block chat replies, and missing config/session/transcript state leaves the session unchanged. Runtime contract risk is limited to the unmerged plugin runtime facade and after-reply background title projection/persistence path; it does not change the model-consumed transcript source of truth before the assistant reply. No dependency or lockfile changes are included.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Real browser/gateway proof plus focused unit regression tests, typecheck, lint, and diff whitespace check.
- Target test files: `src/auto-reply/reply/session-title-generator.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/conversation-label-generator.test.ts`, `src/plugins/runtime/index.test.ts`, `src/plugins/runtime/runtime-model-auth.runtime.test.ts`.
- Real proof artifacts recorded: `round4-ui-proof.md`, `round4-ui-proof.json`, `round4-ui-proof-browser-console.log`, `round4-ui-proof-session-list.png`, `mock-openai-compatible-provider-requests.jsonl`, and `openclaw-dev-gateway-round4-ui-mock-port19034.log`.
- Fresh verification artifacts recorded: `round5-focused-tests.log`, `round5-check-test-types.log`, `round5-lint.log`, `round5-diff-check.log`, `round6-verification.md`, `round6-pnpm-install.log`, `round6-deadcode-ci.log`, `round6-focused-tests.log`, `round6-check-test-types.log`, `round6-lint.log`, and `round6-diff-check.log`.
- Scenario locked in: Long first JSONL user records do not block later turns from reaching the title threshold; generated titles persist as `autoTitle`; `sessions.list` exposes the generated title to Control UI; title generation uses the persisted runtime session model; title-model input is capped before the call; manual/subject-titled sessions do not disclose transcript excerpts to a background title request; and plugin runtime auth wrappers strip credential selectors.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It covers the exact scanner defect, input-cap blocker, model-routing blocker, auth-wrapper blocker, privacy blocker, and user-visible Control UI display path without widening the feature beyond its existing explicit opt-in gate.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The title generator made threshold, routing, and input-boundary decisions at the wrong boundaries: it previously treated arbitrary byte chunks as JSONL records, reconstructed title-model identity from partial fields instead of the canonical session model resolver, and could pass unbounded early transcript text into the title model. The plugin runtime model auth facade also exposed internal credential selector fields to plugin callers.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The scanner consumes complete transcript lines, model routing uses the same resolver as the session reply path, titled sessions return before transcript reads/model calls, title-model input is capped before invocation, and plugin runtime wrappers strip credential selectors at the facade boundary.

- **Observed result after fix:** A real browser opened the Control UI, connected through the page's `GatewayBrowserClient`, confirmed `sessionTitle.enabled`, created a session, sent one 11,297-character first user message plus two later turns, waited for all three agent runs, confirmed each assistant reply marker in `chat.history`, observed durable `autoTitle: Nebula Banana Notebook`, fetched `sessions.list` with `derivedTitle: Nebula Banana Notebook`, and captured a screenshot showing `Nebula Banana Notebook` in the Control UI recent session list. Focused unit tests prove the title model input is capped, long-first-record counting still reaches the title threshold, runtime session model routing still works, manual/subject-titled sessions do not disclose transcript excerpts, and plugin runtime auth wrappers strip credential selectors.
- **What was not tested:** The successful browser proof used a local OpenAI-compatible mock provider rather than a paid external provider because available ZTE and DeepSeek credentials failed with auth/billing errors. No live Mantis/Desktop proof was captured. The mock proof still exercises the real gateway, Control UI, WebSocket RPC client, chat send/wait/history path, title generator, durable session store, and `sessions.list` display path.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** This author update addresses the outstanding waiting-on-author work; the label itself is controlled by maintainers/bots.
- **RF-002 / RF-006:** `session-title-generator.ts` now builds a bounded title-model excerpt before calling `generateConversationLabel`: max 1,000 chars per user message and max 4,000 chars total. `session-title-generator.test.ts` now proves both long-first-record threshold counting and the total input cap.
- **RF-003 / RF-004:** Broader sign-off remains a maintainer decision. This update keeps `sessionTitle.enabled` explicit opt-in, narrows the transcript excerpt sent to the title model, and documents the selected cap and remaining external-provider/Mantis proof gap.
- **RF-005:** `createRuntimeModelAuth()` no longer forwards plugin-supplied `agentDir`, `profileId`, `preferredProfile`, or `lockedProfile` into `getRuntimeAuthForModel`; the public plugin runtime type now exposes only `model`, optional `cfg`, and `workspaceDir` for that facade. `index.test.ts` proves `agentDir`, `profileId`, `preferredProfile`, `lockedProfile`, and `store` are stripped.
- **RF-007:** Ran the requested focused Vitest command covering `src/plugins/runtime/index.test.ts`, `src/plugins/runtime/runtime-model-auth.runtime.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/session-title-generator.test.ts`, and `src/auto-reply/reply/conversation-label-generator.test.ts`; 4 files / 37 tests passed.
- **RF-008:** `git diff --check` passed with no output.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: compatibility`, `merge-risk: auth-provider`, `merge-risk: security-boundary`, plus session-state and message-delivery surfaces in the diff.
- **Risk explanation:** The broader PR adds a default-off `sessionTitle` config surface, durable `autoTitle` metadata, and background title model calls. This update narrows the background title input, uses the session's canonical selected model, fixes the long-record scanner, and prevents plugin runtime callers from steering credential selectors through the model auth facade.
- **Why acceptable:** Generation still requires `sessionTitle.enabled: true`; manual `displayName`, channel `subject`, and existing `autoTitle` prevent any title-model transcript read; title input is capped before the model call; the scanner uses complete transcript lines; and real Control UI proof confirms the gateway/session-list display path. Maintainers still own final sign-off on the broader config/privacy/auth-provider boundaries.

## Maintainer-ready confidence

- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for this contributor update: the current author-side code blockers are fixed, real Control UI proof is refreshed, the prior remote `check-dependencies` failure was attributed to a mainline unused-file allowlist gap and passes after `sync-main`, and focused tests, test typecheck, lint, and diff whitespace checks pass. The broader merge decision still needs maintainer sign-off for the existing config/privacy/auth-provider risk labels.
- **Patch quality notes:** The latest production changes are narrow: cap the existing title-model excerpt, remove plugin-facing credential selector forwarding, use the existing canonical session model resolver, return before transcript reads for already titled sessions, and keep complete-line transcript scanning for long JSONL records. The existing broad catches/default returns are intentional non-blocking behavior for a background title feature: failed title generation must not block chat replies, and missing config/session/transcript state leaves the session unchanged. Runtime contract risk is limited to the unmerged plugin runtime facade and after-reply background title projection/persistence path; it does not change the model-consumed transcript source of truth before the assistant reply. No dependency or lockfile changes are included.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Real browser/gateway proof plus focused unit regression tests, typecheck, lint, and diff whitespace check.
- Target test files: `src/auto-reply/reply/session-title-generator.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/conversation-label-generator.test.ts`, `src/plugins/runtime/index.test.ts`, `src/plugins/runtime/runtime-model-auth.runtime.test.ts`.
- Real proof artifacts recorded: `round4-ui-proof.md`, `round4-ui-proof.json`, `round4-ui-proof-browser-console.log`, `round4-ui-proof-session-list.png`, `mock-openai-compatible-provider-requests.jsonl`, and `openclaw-dev-gateway-round4-ui-mock-port19034.log`.
- Fresh verification artifacts recorded: `round5-focused-tests.log`, `round5-check-test-types.log`, `round5-lint.log`, `round5-diff-check.log`, `round6-verification.md`, `round6-pnpm-install.log`, `round6-deadcode-ci.log`, `round6-focused-tests.log`, `round6-check-test-types.log`, `round6-lint.log`, and `round6-diff-check.log`.
- Scenario locked in: Long first JSONL user records do not block later turns from reaching the title threshold; generated titles persist as `autoTitle`; `sessions.list` exposes the generated title to Control UI; title generation uses the persisted runtime session model; title-model input is capped before the call; manual/subject-titled sessions do not disclose transcript excerpts to a background title request; and plugin runtime auth wrappers strip credential selectors.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It covers the exact scanner defect, input-cap blocker, model-routing blocker, auth-wrapper blocker, privacy blocker, and user-visible Control UI display path without widening the feature beyond its existing explicit opt-in gate.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The title generator made threshold, routing, and input-boundary decisions at the wrong boundaries: it previously treated arbitrary byte chunks as JSONL records, reconstructed title-model identity from partial fields instead of the canonical session model resolver, and could pass unbounded early transcript text into the title model. The plugin runtime model auth facade also exposed internal credential selector fields to plugin callers.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The scanner consumes complete transcript lines, model routing uses the same resolver as the session reply path, titled sessions return before transcript reads/model calls, title-model input is capped before invocation, and plugin runtime wrappers strip credential selectors at the facade boundary.
